he set his right foot upon the sea/left foot on the earth. John speaks three times of the angel standing on sea and land (10:2, 5, 8). A similar expression is found in D&C 88:110, where the order of "land" and "sea" is reversed. "Earth" and "sea" appear to indicate the total existence of the telestial world (Ex. 20:4,

Seal not the sayings of the prophecy of this book. Daniel was given a vision and told to seal it up "to the time of the end" (Dan. 8:26; 12:4, 9)-it was not the Lord's purpose for the world to know the things Daniel received. Earlier in John's vision, he is told to "seal up those things which the seven thunders uttered, and write them not" (10:4). Perhaps the time has not yet come for these…

release the Bible! Bill Gates can decide whether to release software he wrote, but he cannot decide whether to release all of the world's software that is already widely dispersed and that he did not write or even necessarily have a copy of!!! John would have failed at sealing the Bible just as Daniel would have had he tried to seal the Bible when the Lord told him to "seal the book" (Dan. 12:4).…
